name: Sri
description_short: SRI Federal Credit Union is a member-owned, not-for-profit credit union founded in 1957 by SRI International employees, offering competitive savings rates, loans, and mortgages with NCUA insurance.
description_long:
SRI Federal Credit Union was established in 1957 when seven SRI International employees pooled their savings to create a cooperative lending institution. What began as a small workplace credit union has grown into a nationwide organization serving members worldwide. The credit union maintains its original mission of supporting innovators and those working to improve the world, reinvesting financial success back into members rather than shareholders.

SRI FCU offers a comprehensive range of financial products including savings accounts with tiered rates (3.50% APYE on first $5,000 in primary savings), certificate of deposit products (advertised at 4.08% APYE for 9-month terms), home equity lines of credit (6.75% APR variable up to 80% LTV), auto loans (4.99% APR variable with 0.26% discount for zero-emission vehicles), mortgages for primary and secondary homes, and credit cards with rewards programs. The organization provides online banking, ATM access, and skip-a-pay options for budget management.

As a democratically controlled credit union, SRI FCU distinguishes itself through member governance and dividend reinvestment rather than profit extraction. The institution emphasizes ethical practices and commitment to its membership base rather than external shareholders. Their focus on serving innovation-minded individuals aligns with the SRI International heritage, and they actively support community engagement through memorial scholarship programs and financial education content addressing topics like P2P/A2A scams.

SRI Federal Credit Union is a legitimate, NCUA-insured financial institution with transparent rate disclosures and standard credit union operations. Members benefit from competitive rates and member-centric governance. The main limitation is that membership eligibility appears tied to SRI International affiliation or established networks, and detailed membership requirements are not extensively outlined on the public-facing website.
services: Savings accounts with tiered interest rates
Home equity lines of credit (HELOC) for primary, secondary, and non-owner occupied properties
Credit cards with Visa uChoose Rewards
Financial education and fraud prevention resources
pros: NCUA insurance covering deposits up to $250,000 minimum
Competitive savings rates with tiered structure (3.50% APYE on first $5K)
0.26% rate discount on auto loans for zero-emission vehicles
Democratic member governance with profits reinvested in members
cons: Membership eligibility requirements not clearly detailed on website
Variable rate products subject to rate increases (HELOC, auto loans)
No mention of fee structure beyond reference to 'low fees'
Limited information about lending approval criteria
Geographic service area and branch/ATM availability not comprehensively documented
best_for: SRI International employees and their families seeking member-owned financial services
Individuals prioritizing ethical finance and reinvestment of profits to members
EV owners looking for auto loan rate discounts
Members seeking competitive savings rates and NCUA protection
diagnosis: SRI Federal Credit Union is best for SRI International-affiliated members and those seeking member-owned financial institution benefits with competitive rates and NCUA insurance protection. The primary caveat is that specific membership eligibility criteria are not transparently disclosed on the public website, so prospective members should verify qualification before applying.
typical_results_timeline: Membership approval and account opening typically takes 1-3 business days. Loan decisions are usually faster than traditional banks.
